content.full_text:
T-82-140

TO: [ ]JEANNE      [ ] JAY                    FROM: Irm

    [ ]LYNN        [ ] BARBARA               DATE: 7/19/82

    [ ]RICH        [ ] BOB S.

    [ ]HELEN       [ ] OTHER_____Legal_____

[ ]TELEPHONE ADVICE - SEE BELOW

[ ]CORRESPONDENCE ADVICE - SEE ATTACHED DRAFT
    (IF YOU HAVE ANY COMMENTS, NOTIFY ADVISOR WITHIN 1 DAY)

FILE LOCATION: 82013 (b) & 18225 (b)

INCLUDE IN ADVICE PACKAGE:        YES [ ]        NO [ ]

_________________________________________________________________

CALLER: Bruce Bishop and Neil Morgan (and Keith Boyer)

REPRESENTING: San Diego Tribune (and San Diego Elections)

PHONE NUMBER: (714) 236-1661 299-3131 X1358 (& 565-5520)

QUESTION: Neil Morgan, editor of the San Diego Tribune, printed a copy of
         ballot petition along with an editorial urging people to sign it
         petition and return it to the Tribune. The postage will be paid
         by the newspaper. What reporting requirements does the newspaper
         incur? (No coordination has occurred between the newspaper and
         the committee primarily formed for the measure.)

ANSWER:   The newspaper has no requirement to report the costs for the ed-
         itorial and reprinting of the petition pursuant to 2 Cal Admin
         Code Sec. 18225 (b) (4) (A).

         Additional costs incurred such as the return postage or employee
         spending 10% or more of their compensated time on coordination
         toward                  would accrue the $500 independent expenditure committee requirement and the paper should file as such a committee at the point
         that its reportable independent expenditures total $500.

_________________________________________________________________

sections.question:
Neil Morgan, editor of the San Diego Tribune, printed a copy of ballot petition along with an editorial urging people to sign the petition and return it to the Tribune. The postage will be paid by the newspaper. What reporting requirements does the newspaper incur? (No coordination has occurred between the newspaper and the committee primarily formed for the measure.)

ANSWER:
The newspaper has no requirement to report the costs for the editorial and reprinting of the petition pursuant to 2 CA1 Admin Code Sec. 18225 (b) (4) (A).

Additional costs incurred such as the return postage or employee spending 10% or more of their compensated time on coordination toward would accrue the $500 independent expenditure committee requirement and the paper should file as such a committee at the point that its reportable independent expenditures total $500.
